items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud C
id: 179f0d28-0165-5de7-b417-e97f47ee13b8
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud Credentials (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud Credentials
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/23
modified: 2026/07/23
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud Credentials — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-23T17:32:57Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud Credentials", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX Hackers Abuse GitHub Actions to Exploit cPanel and WHM Servers and Steal Cloud Credentials Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2694565; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-23, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Qilin Ransomware Affiliates Abuse CVE-2026-0257 to Gain Unauthorized VPN Access
items.description: Qilin ransomware exploits the PAN-OS GlobalProtect flaw CVE-2026-0257 to gain unauthorized VPN access to unpatched networks. Arctic Wolf researchers warn that the Qilin ransomware gang is exploiting the critical PAN-OS GlobalProtect vulnerability CVE-2026-0257 to compromise corporate networks. CVE-2026-0257 is a PAN-OS authentication bypass vulnerability affecting GlobalProtect portals and gateways. Palo Alto Networks addressed the vulnerability on May […]

items.title: pypdf: Possible infinite loop for not terminated inline images (ASCII85 and ASCIIHex filter)
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to an infinite loop. This requires parsing the content stream of a page with a not terminated inline image, as done when extracting the page text for example. It only affects the ASCII85 and ASCIIHex filters.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.2](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.2).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3892](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3892).
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 7.6/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: pypdf: Possible long runtimes for repeated malformed cross-reference entries
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to long runtimes. This requires cross-reference streams with repeated malformed cross-reference streams.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.0](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.0).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3887](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3887).

items.title: pypdf: Possible large memory usage for wrong image dimensions
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to large memory usage. This requires loading images where the declared size values are much too large compared to the actual data.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.0](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.0).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3888](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3888).
